## Step 4: Merge your academic writing portfolio

_Your annotated outline is looking great! :mortar_board:_

You've defined your research question, drafted with AI and reflected on it, and built an annotated argument outline. The final step is to merge your work into `main` — signifying that you are satisfied with the quality and integrity of your academic writing portfolio.

Before you merge, take a moment to review all three files as a set:

- **`research-question.md`** — Is your question focused and answerable? Does your hypothesis follow logically?
- **`draft-with-reflection.md`** — Does your revised draft represent your actual argument? Are your reflections honest about what the AI got wrong?
- **`outline.md`** — Does your outline tell a coherent story? Could a reader follow the argument from introduction to conclusion?

If you spot anything to improve, make the changes in your `academic-writing` branch before merging.

### :keyboard: Activity: Merge your pull request

1. Navigate to your open pull request **Academic writing portfolio**.

2. Review the **Files changed** tab to confirm all three files look correct:
   - `research-question.md`
   - `draft-with-reflection.md`
   - `outline.md`

3. Return to the **Conversation** tab.

4. Click **Merge pull request**, then **Confirm merge**.

5. Mona will post the course review. Give her a moment and refresh the page.

> **Reflection prompt 💡**: After merging, ask yourself — in this exercise, when did AI help you most? When did it mislead you? What would you do differently next time? These answers are the judgment you've co-evolved.

<details>
<summary>Having trouble? 🤷</summary><br/>

If you don't get feedback, here are some things to check:
- Make sure you are merging the `academic-writing` branch into `main`, not the other way around.
- Confirm the pull request title is `Academic writing portfolio`.

</details>
